steps.text: STEP 1: Make connections by dragging nodes from apparatus to the circuit.  power supply to the circuit, 1 to 9 and 2 to 10. Connect ammeters, 3 to 11 and 4 to 12, 5 to 13 and 6 to 14, and 7 to 15 and 8 to 16, or connect any ammeter to any resistance in circuit. Click on the label to delete all the connections for the corresponding nodes.
STEP 2: Check your connections by clicking on the CHECK button.
STEP 3: Set the values of resistances R1, R2 and R3 by adjusting the sliders on left. These values will remain constant throughout the experiment.
STEP 4: Click the Power button to turn on the power supply.
STEP 5: Vary the voltage value by moving the voltage slider to the right side. The readings on the ammeter will change accordingly.
STEP 6: Click on the ADD button to add the readings to the observation table.
STEP 7: Repeat step 6 until 6 readings are recorded. A minimum of 6 readings is required to plot the graph.
STEP 8: Click on the PLOT button to display the graph.
STEP 9: Click on the Generate Report button below the graph to open the experiment report.
STEP 10: Use the report page controls to print or download the generated report.
STEP 11: Click on RESET button to refresh the page.
